concerns.text: T045 instructs shuffling scores 'among User-Track pairs' but FR-007 requires shuffling 'among tracks' while preserving User-Track grouping. The task description contradicts the spec's block-permutation logic, making the implementation non-deterministic regarding the null distribution construction.

T045 defines output columns as 'iteration, statistic, p_value'. A single p-value per iteration is logically impossible; p-values are derived from the full null distribution (1000 iterations). The task lacks a concrete deliverable for the null distribution itself, preventing verification of the permutation test.

T044 describes re-matching and re-aggregating but omits the explicit requirement to re-run the ingestion/fallback logic (T023/T015) if new thresholds alter dataset composition. Without this, the sensitivity analysis may use inconsistent data states, violating FR-006's 'entire process' requirement.

T045 specifies 1000 iterations but fails to mandate pinning the random seed for the permutation process. This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) and makes the specific null distribution non-deterministic and unrepeatable.

T012a relies on a local metadata field `source_type == 'human'` to verify AMT data integrity. Constitution Principle II requires external citations to be verified by the Reference-Validator Agent against primary sources. This task bypasses the mandated verification mechanism, making the 'human-collected' claim unverified and the task non-compliant.

T027 describes filtering 'tracks with high exposure but no memory cues' without defining the 'high exposure' threshold or explicitly stating the filter applies at the User-Track Pair level (per EC-002). The lack of a concrete threshold value or aggregation level makes the task ambiguous and non-deterministic.
